**THE INTERVIEW - C1 LEVEL (5 minutes)**
**Target:** 20-30 words per sentence, complex structures
**Connectors:** Sophisticated connectors (nevertheless, furthermore, consequently, in addition, despite the fact that)
**Word count target:** ~650-700 words

---

Carlos sat at his computer, staring blankly at the screen as yet another rejection email materialized in his inbox, marking the 46th time he had been told that his qualifications were not quite right or that his English proficiency was insufficient for the position. This particular rejection felt especially crushing because it came from a company he had been particularly excited about, and with his savings rapidly dwindling and rent due in two weeks, tomorrow's interview represented not just another opportunity but quite literally his last chance to secure employment before facing financial disaster.

When Carlos had first arrived in America six months earlier, he had been filled with optimism and determination, believing wholeheartedly that his work ethic and willingness to learn would overcome any language barriers he might encounter. He had dedicated countless hours to improving his English, enrolling in evening classes, practicing conversations with native speakers he met at coffee shops, and consuming English media voraciously in an attempt to immerse himself in the language. Despite these extensive efforts, however, the job market had proven far more unforgiving than he had anticipated, with employers consistently emphasizing the importance of flawless communication skills over other qualifications.

The night before what he knew would be his final interview opportunity, Carlos found himself unable to sleep, his mind racing through potential questions and rehearsed responses that he had meticulously prepared. He had spent hours crafting what he believed were perfect answers to common interview questions, memorizing industry-specific vocabulary, and practicing his pronunciation until his throat felt raw. Nevertheless, as he lay awake in the darkness, a nagging sense of inadequacy gnawed at him, and he could not shake the feeling that no amount of preparation would be sufficient to overcome the fundamental barrier of his imperfect English.

The following morning, Carlos awoke before dawn, his anxiety having prevented him from achieving anything resembling restful sleep. He carefully selected his best suit, which he had taken to a professional cleaner the previous week, and spent an inordinate amount of time ensuring that every detail of his appearance was impeccable. Standing before the mirror, he attempted to project confidence through his reflection, repeating affirmations about his capabilities and worth, but the tremor in his hands betrayed the fear that was consuming him from within.

As Carlos navigated the morning traffic toward the downtown office building where his interview would take place, he could feel his heart pounding against his ribcage with such intensity that he worried the interviewers might be able to hear it when he entered the room. The building itself was an imposing glass structure that seemed to stretch endlessly toward the sky, and as he stepped through the automatic doors into the polished marble lobby, he felt small and insignificant, like an imposter who did not belong in such a professional environment.

The elevator ride to the 15th floor felt interminable, each floor number that lit up on the display panel seeming to mark another moment of his impending judgment. When the doors finally opened, Carlos found himself in a corridor lined with closed office doors, and he followed the directions he had been given until he reached the conference room where three interviewers were already seated around a polished wooden table, their expressions neutral and professional as they gestured for him to take the empty chair across from them.

The initial portion of the interview proceeded smoothly enough, with Carlos responding competently to questions about his educational background and previous work experience. However, when one of the interviewers, a woman with kind eyes and a warm smile, asked him to tell them about himself, something entirely unexpected occurred. Despite having rehearsed this exact question dozens of times the previous night, Carlos's mind went completely blank, as if someone had erased all the carefully memorized responses from his memory. He sat there in stunned silence, feeling the weight of three pairs of eyes upon him, and in that moment, he experienced a wave of panic so intense that he genuinely considered excusing himself and fleeing the room.

Instead of giving in to his instinct to escape, however, Carlos closed his eyes and took several deliberate, measured breaths, forcing himself to slow down his racing thoughts. In that moment of quiet desperation, he found himself reflecting on the journey that had brought him to this point—the sacrifices his family had made to support his dreams, the countless hours he had spent studying English when he could have been relaxing, and the unwavering determination that had carried him through 46 rejections without completely losing hope. He realized that he had been approaching this interview all wrong, trying to present a polished, perfect version of himself rather than allowing his authentic self to shine through.

When Carlos opened his eyes and looked directly at the interviewers, he made a conscious decision to abandon his rehearsed script and speak from his heart instead. He began slowly, his words initially hesitant, as he told them about his journey from his small hometown to this moment, about the challenges he had faced in learning a new language while simultaneously trying to build a new life, and about the dreams that had sustained him through every setback. He did not attempt to use sophisticated vocabulary or complex grammatical structures; instead, he spoke with raw honesty and genuine passion, allowing his determination and work ethic to come through in the sincerity of his words.

The transformation in the room was palpable as the interviewers leaned forward, their expressions shifting from professional neutrality to genuine interest. They began asking follow-up questions, and Carlos found himself answering them with increasing confidence, sharing not just his strengths but also his areas of growth and his commitment to continuous learning. He demonstrated his passion for his field, his resilience in the face of adversity, and his understanding that success is not about perfection but about persistence and authenticity.

As the interview concluded and Carlos shook hands with each of the interviewers, thanking them sincerely for their time and consideration, he felt a profound sense of accomplishment that had nothing to do with whether he would receive a job offer. He had faced his greatest fear—the fear of not being good enough—and had discovered that his value lay not in perfect English but in his character, his determination, and his willingness to be vulnerable and authentic. Two days later, when the email arrived offering him the position, Carlos understood that sometimes the greatest strength we can bring to any situation is simply the courage to be ourselves, imperfections and all.
